violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (nozzles at the beverage machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the of the areas behind equipment in the front food prep area observed some droppings. If there is stil activity after cleaning, establishment shall increase pest control service to twice a month. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The ceiling in the back prep area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. -Per interview of PIC, the ceiling tiles will be replaced once renovation starts towards the end of 2025. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Ceiling tiles in the back prep area observed in poor repair (hole/gap). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Openings shall be sealed to prevent entry of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

An employee was observed washing their hands at the food prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Designated hand washing sink reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Observed the hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by C02 canister tanks. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -In addition, the food-contact surfaces of the nozzles at the beverage machine observed with residue. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the slicer and nozzles during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The presence of rodent activity is not controlled as evidenced by droppings (old and new) on food contact surfaces (food wrap) and underneath the front cold case and the back prep table. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Staff voluntarily disposed of the contaminated food wraps during this inspection. -Establishment must contact pest control. -Proof of service must be provided to inspector upon the next reinspection date. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Observed insect device hanging on food prep area and above the food prep sink. (B) Insect control devices shall be installed so that: (1) The devices are not located over a FOOD preparation area; and (2) Dead insects and insect fragments are prevented from being impelled onto or falling on exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; and unwrapped SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. PIC removed insect device to designated area away from food prep area and food contact areas. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen fan and vent in walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The ceiling in the back prep area is constructed of porous tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Per PIC establishment will undergo a renovation sometime this year. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The ceiling at the back prep and storage area observed with an unsealed opening. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. -Per PIC establishment is working on getting it sealed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by new rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC stated pest control will be increased to 2x monthly. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Food contact surfaces of the deli slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. PIC to instruct all employees on proper sanitizing procedures. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was Correct By: 11/14/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings throughout the facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is currently using pest control 1x monthly. Establishment to clean all droppings to aid in monitoring activity. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
